info.name: BreachLab Admin API
info.description:
Admin endpoints under /api/v1/admin/*. All routes require an authenticated user with role=ADMIN. Auth is via Clerk session JWT in the Authorization header.

Set the collection variables before running:
- baseUrl (e.g. http://localhost:3000/api/v1)
- adminToken (Clerk session JWT for an ADMIN user)
- challengeId / userId as needed

body.raw:
{
  "title": "SQL Injection 101",
  "description": "Practice classic SQLi against a vulnerable login form.",
  "scenario": "You're a junior pentester. Bypass the login of the staging app and exfiltrate the admin email.",
  "domain": "APPSEC",
  "difficulty": "BEGINNER",
  "passScore": 70,
  "objectives": [
    "Bypass the login",
    "Extract the admin email"
  ],
  "tools": ["Burp Suite", "sqlmap"],
  "isFree": true,
  "points": 100,
  "questions": [
    {
      "text": "What payload bypassed authentication?",
      "answerKey": "' OR 1=1 --",
      "order": 1
    },
    {
      "text": "What is the admin email?",
      "answerKey": "admin@breachlab.test",
      "order": 2
    }
  ]
}

request.description:
Creates a challenge with at least one question. Required: title, description, scenario, domain, difficulty, questions[]. Optional: passScore (1-100), objectives, tools, isFree (default true), points (default 100).

domain: APPSEC | SOC | NETWORK | WIRESHARK | GRC
difficulty: BEGINNER | INTERMEDIATE

body.raw:
{
  "title": "SQL Injection 101 (Revised)",
  "description": "Updated description.",
  "scenario": "Updated scenario text.",
  "passScore": 75,
  "objectives": ["Bypass the login", "Extract the admin email", "Document the vector"],
  "tools": ["Burp Suite", "sqlmap", "curl"],
  "domain": "APPSEC",
  "difficulty": "INTERMEDIATE",
  "isFree": false,
  "points": 150
}

request.description: Partial update — every field is optional, but at least one must be provided. Cannot edit: id, fileKey, createdAt, updatedAt, questions. Returns 404 if the challenge is missing or soft-deleted.
Smallest valid PATCH — flip a single field.
Soft-deletes the challenge (sets deletedAt) and removes the file from S3 if one exists. List/detail endpoints will hide it after this call. Returns 404 if missing or already deleted.

Returns a short-lived (5 min) presigned S3 PUT URL. Allowed contentType: application/zip or application/x-zip-compressed. Max size: 500 MB. sizeBytes is optional but recommended for client-side validation.

Confirms a file uploaded via the presigned URL. The key must be the one returned from /presign and must start with challenges/{id}/. The server verifies the object exists in S3 (HeadObject) before persisting fileKey on the challenge. If a previous file existed, it is deleted from S3.

query.description: Case-insensitive match against username OR email.

request.description: Paginated list of users sorted by createdAt desc. clerkId and paystackCustomerId are excluded from the response.
Search by username or email (case-insensitive contains).

Aggregate dashboard stats: totals (users, proUsers, challenges, submissions, passedSubmissions), recentSignups (last 10 users), recentSubmissions (last 10 with user + challenge). passedSubmissions excludes submissions against soft-deleted challenges.
